data_source: 9 completed match scorecards (04/18–06/20/2026) + season aggregate stats
match_approach: Troy Titans sit 13th in Group B (3W/6L, NRR −1.09) — a must-win for WW. Our last four games produced 92, 122, 122, and 111 runs batting first; Troy's poor NRR suggests their bowling leaks runs. Win the toss and bat. Set 110+, then unleash Ajinkya Pradhan and Yuvraj Rajpurohit to exploit their brittle batting lineup.
form_notes: WW have won 4 consecutive matches (Princeton Walk, Plainsboro Hunters, MKCC Weekenders, PRO SQUAD). Vinit Keni is in brilliant form (45 and 55* in last two innings). Vivek Singh's role has shifted — he is most dangerous when batting 6–7, not at the top.

batting_order.name: Vedant Kumar
batting_order.reason: The permanent opener. Has three 20+ scores (22, 21, 27) and provides the early platform. His 116+ SR in those big innings shows he can attack when needed.

batting_order.name: Yogen Phanse
batting_order.reason: Opens or bats #2 depending on lineup. His explosive SR (154–158 in his best outings) creates quick momentum. Also bowls containment in middle overs. Keep him early so his big-hitting phase overlaps with the powerplay.

batting_order.name: Vinit Keni
batting_order.reason: The cornerstone. When Keni stays in, WW win. His last two innings — 45 and 55* — anchored totals of 122 and 111. He is NOT slow: SR of 145 and 134 in those games. Protect him at #3 and build the innings around him.

batting_order.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
batting_order.reason: Vice-captain and #1 bowler. Bats 4–6 depending on game state. His retired-hurt 20 vs Express Rangers and 15 vs PRO SQUAD show he can build a platform. Bats here to conserve energy for full bowling quota.

batting_order.name: Anurag Dabee
batting_order.reason: Steady contributor who finishes games (19* not out in the PRO SQUAD win). Good running between wickets, steady under pressure.

batting_order.name: Vivek Singh
batting_order.scorecard_form: EARLY ORDER FAILURES: pos3–4 scores = 5,0,5,0,0,1 (6 innings). MOVED DOWN: pos6–7 scores = 11*(14), 52*(27), 18(14) — 3 innings, 81 runs
batting_order.reason: CRITICAL INSIGHT FROM SCORECARDS: Vivek consistently fails when batting top 4 (5,0,5,0,0,1 across 6 innings). He is MATCH-WINNING at positions 6–7, including a match-saving 52* off 27 balls (5 sixes, SR=192!) vs Plainsboro Hunters. He must be held back. Float him to 5 only if WW are 50+ in 8 overs and wickets are intact.

batting_order.name: Malav Shah
batting_order.scorecard_form: 29(15) 4×6 SR=193 vs JC Warriors; 4*(1); 0(4); 10*(6) SR=167 vs Plainsboro; 1(4); DNB vs PRO SQUAD
batting_order.reason: The sixer machine. When he gets any kind of delivery in the final 6 overs, he hits it out of the park — 4 sixes in 15 balls against JC Warriors. Move him UP to #7 (currently bats 8–9 and often doesn't face enough balls). His explosive SR (146.67 aggregate, 193 peak) is wasted at #9.

batting_order.name: Siddhant Kumar
batting_order.reason: Useful lower-order bat who can hit — 14* off 9 with 2 sixes vs MKCC. Bats 8 and bowls in middle overs.

batting_order.name: Santhosh Suresh Kumar
batting_order.reason: Can contribute useful late-innings runs. Bowls useful overs (2-1 vs PRO SQUAD for 5 runs).

batting_order.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
batting_order.reason: Key bowling asset — bats low to preserve full energy for 4-over bowling allocation.

batting_order.name: Abhinav Tyagi
batting_order.reason: Pure bowling asset. Bats tail with licence to swing hard.

bowling_rotation.powerplay_note: PowerPlay = 4 overs. Captain can take it ANYTIME between overs 1–8 — this is a strategic weapon, not a fixed block. Best used when opposition's best batsmen are in to force errors under fielding restrictions.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: WW's best bowler by distance. Economy under 4.0 in 5 of 7 appearances. His 1.00 econ vs Em Sports (4-1-4-1) and 1.50 vs Princeton Walk (4-1-6-2) are elite performances. Opens the attack.

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.match_data: Econ 2.83 season avg; 1.00 econ vs Princeton Walk (3-1-3-2 — 2 wickets for 3 runs!)
bowlers.reason: The surprise PowerPlay weapon. When available, Rathi generates dot-ball pressure that sets up the rest of the innings. His match vs Princeton Walk (3 overs, 3 runs, 2 wickets) was extraordinary. Use him whenever he is playing.

powerplay.alternative: If Namit Rathi is not playing, use Malav Shah for the 2nd PowerPlay over (4.00 economy, tight lines).

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.reason: The containment workhorse. Economy between 3.67–4.25 across 16 overs in the last four games. He concedes runs at under run-a-ball through the middle phase — exactly what's needed. His wickets are a bonus; his VALUE is the pressure he builds through dot balls.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.reason: Dangerous and match-winning when on — his 4-over spell vs MKCC (3 wickets for 9 runs!) was the difference. Variable economy means captain should watch for expensive starts and rotate him if losing it. Excellent death-over option when he has rhythm.

bowlers.name: Siddhant Kumar
bowlers.reason: Partnership-breaker but USE WITH CAUTION. When good (2.67 vs MKCC, 5.25 vs Em Sports) he adds value. When off (17.00 vs JC Warriors, 11.50 vs Sreeshti) he's a liability. Cap at 2 overs; pull him immediately if expensive.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Non-negotiable at the death. His slower ball and variation make him the hardest to score off in the last 2 overs. ALWAYS save his remaining allocation for 13–16.

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.reason: Death specialist when his rhythm is right. The MKCC game proved his quality. Pair him with Pradhan for a two-pronged death attack.

bowlers.name: Abhinav Tyagi
bowlers.match_data: 3 wkts vs Sreeshti; takes wickets but expensive (6.50 avg econ)
bowlers.reason: Raw pace can surprise at the death if fielding are set. Use as a 1-over option if the partnership needs breaking and both Pradhan/Yuvraj overs are done.

economy_first_bowlers.note: These bowlers may not always top the wickets column but build pressure through dot balls that lead to wickets for others.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.note: Economy AND wickets — the complete package

bowlers.name: Namit Rathi
bowlers.note: Best economy in the squad — forces batsmen into errors

bowlers.name: Malav Shah
bowlers.note: Concedes under run-a-ball — crucial containment role

bowlers.name: Yuvraj Rajpurohit
bowlers.note: High wickets AND good economy — use his full quota

key_matchups: Ajinkya Pradhan bowls his FULL 4-over quota — 17 wickets at 3.60 economy is the bedrock of WW's bowling. Never split his overs unevenly.

Vivek Singh MUST bat 6 or 7 — his top-order record (5,0,5,0,0,1 in 6 innings at pos 3–4) versus his lower-order record (11*, 52*, 18 in 3 innings at pos 6–7) is night and day. His 52* off 27 balls vs Plainsboro won that game.
Vinit Keni is the batting backbone — his 45 and 55* in the last two games prove he is in the best form of the season. Build the innings around him.
Malav Shah belongs at #7 — 29(15) with 4 sixes, 10*(6) — he is wasted coming in at #9 with 1 ball left.
Take the PowerPlay at overs 5–8 if Troy Titans' openers are set and threatening — use Pradhan + Rathi under restrictions to force false shots.

scorecard_insights.vivek_singh_pattern: Positions 3–4: 5,0,5,0,0,1 = 11 runs in 6 innings. Positions 6–7: 11*,52*,18 = 81 runs in 3 innings. Data is conclusive.
scorecard_insights.vinit_keni_anchor: When Keni bats 20+ balls, WW wins. His departure triggers collapses (see M4 Monroe 21 all out). Protect him at #3.
scorecard_insights.pradhan_economy_peaks: Vs Em Sports: 4-1-4-1 (econ 1.00). Vs Princeton Walk: 4-1-6-2 (econ 1.50). Vs Plainsboro: 3-0-7-3 (econ 2.33). World-class bowling.
scorecard_insights.rathi_powerplay_weapon: Vs Princeton Walk: 3-1-3-2 (econ 1.00 with 2 wickets). Rathi in the PowerPlay is a game-changer — use him every match he plays.
scorecard_insights.malav_shah_containment: Last 4 bowling outings: 4.00, 3.75, 3.67, 4.25 econ — the most consistent containment bowler in the squad after Pradhan.
scorecard_insights.collapse_warning: M4 vs Monroe: WW collapsed to 21 all out in 9.1 overs. All batsmen dismissed for 0–6. This shows WW's tail is fragile — protect Keni and Vivek's big-innings partnership.
